title: Include the FROM directive in Docker history Closes #2391 (second attempt)
body:
Author: Diego <diego.canizales1995@gmail.com>
Date:   Fri Aug 7 12:11:31 2020 -0500

    Added reference to the parent image. Signed-off-by: Diego Goytia <diego.canizales1995@gmail.com>

Signed-off-by: Diego <diego.canizales1995@gmail.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

As requested by @TomSweeneyRedHat on #2391, taken from: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1649044

#### How to verify it

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

#2391

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

This is my second pull request. First one was this, with the same changes, but squashed into a single commit: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2514

Please let me know if it completes the requirement, otherwise I'll modify it! 

Thanks.


```release-note

```

comments.body:
Almost there.

You need to fix up you commit. The first line of commit must be < 80 characters long.
Then you need an empty line.

After that you can put your signedby line.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force
Thank you @rhatdan ! There it goes again.
I think I gotta add the bats tests for this to come up ok. I'll work on it.
The bats tests indicate that they are now returning 3 lines rather then 1, check if this is expected after the change, if so then change the number expected to 3 and it should pass the test.
@ironknight78 Could you update this PR and add tests.  We want to get this into the buildah 1.16 release, which is coming up soon.

Of course Dan! I'm working on it. Honestly I'm a little bit stuck on the tests... I know that it is expected that the number of lines of the output to change, so that's why those tests are failing. The thing is that I think that some of the *.bats tests actually check the length of both the RootFS.DiffIDs and the History. Since i'm only appending the name of the parent file to the History, the number of RootFS.DiffIDs is not changing.. so I'm a little bit stuck :(

If you could help me understand what would I have to do that would be awesome @rhatdan 

I know the failing tests are, for example `squash.bats` in the` check_lengths` function:


```
function check_lengths() {
  local image=$1
  local expect=$2

  # matrix test: check given .Docker.* and .OCIv1.* fields in image
  for which in Docker OCIv1; do
    for field in RootFS.DiffIDs History; do
      run_buildah inspect -t image -f "{{len .$which.$field}}" $image
      expect_output "$expect"
    done
  done
}

```


Being called as such:
```

@test "squash" {
        createrandom ${TESTDIR}/randomfile
        run_buildah from scratch
        cid=$output
        image=stage0
        remove=(8 5)
        for stage in $(seq 10) ; do
                run_buildah copy "$cid" ${TESTDIR}/randomfile /layer${stage}
                image=stage${stage}
                if test $stage -eq ${remove[0]} ; then
                        run_buildah mount "$cid"
                        mountpoint=$output
                        rm -f ${mountpoint}/layer${remove[1]}
                fi
                run_buildah commit --signature-policy ${TESTSDIR}/policy.json --rm "$cid" ${image}
                check_lengths $image $stage
                run_buildah from --quiet ${image}
                cid=$output
        done
        run_buildah commit --signature-policy ${TESTSDIR}/policy.json --rm --squash "$cid" squashed

        check_lengths squashed 1

        run_buildah from --quiet squashed
        cid=$output
        run_buildah mount $cid
        mountpoint=$output
        for stage in $(seq 10) ; do
                if test $stage -eq ${remove[1]} ; then
                        if test -e $mountpoint/layer${remove[1]} ; then
                                echo file /layer${remove[1]} should not be there
                                exit 1
                        fi
                        continue
                fi
                cmp $mountpoint/layer${stage} ${TESTDIR}/randomfile
        done
```


So it fails:

```
[+1329s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah inspect -t image -f {{len .Docker.History}} stage1

[+1329s] # 3

[+1329s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv

[+1329s] # #|     FAIL: buildah inspect -t image -f {{len .Docker.History}} stage1

[+1329s] # #| expected: '1'

[+1329s] # #|   actual: '3'

[+1329s] # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

```

@rhatdan @nalind thoughts?
@rhatdan are we holding 1.16 for this?

@nalind will probably have a better suggestion, but I was able to get the test to pass with a bit of a gross change to the squash.bats test file at: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/tests/squash.bats#L14 by changing that from:

```
      expect_output "$expect"
```

to:
```
      if $output != "$expect" && $output != "$((expect + 2))" ; then
        expect_output "$expect"
      fi
```

I don't think we have to hold this for 1.6.
@ironknight78 are you still working on this?
@rhatdan OK, I'll cut a 1.16 this afternoon then.
hey @rhatdan sorry I've been a little off, I was busy with some other stuff! :D I'll add the changes today and fix the commit!

I'm uncertain on how to fix this new issue: It would seem that the manifest is not getting recognized over here: On my local machine if I run ``` diego@ossdevel:~$ podman manifest inspect docker.io/library/nginx Error: error inspect manifest docker.io/library/nginx: loading manifest "docker.io/library/nginx": unable to load manifest list: unsupported format "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json": manifest type not supported ``` And I'm thinking this is related to the manifest unkown issue I'm getting in the CI process: ``` [+0073s] + podman run -i --name in_podman_5436025625378816 -e CIRRUS_USER_PERMISSION=read -e CIRRUS_LAST_GREEN_BUILD_ID=4908807551713280 -e CIRRUS_DEFAULT_BRANCH=master -e CIRRUS_REPO_ID=5138144844840960 -e STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs -e CIRRUS_REPO_FULL_NAME=containers/buildah -e CIRRUS_REPO_OWNER=containers -e CIRRUS_USER_COLLABORATOR=false -e CIRRUS_SHELL=/bin/bash -e CIRRUS_HTTP_CACHE_HOST=127.0.0.1:12321 -e CIRRUS_PR=2520 -e CIRRUS_BASE_BRANCH=master -e CIRRUS_REPO_CLONE_HOST=github.com -e CIRRUS_CI=true -e CIRRUS_BUILD_ID=5436025625378816 -e CIRRUS_CLONE_DEPTH=50 -e CIRRUS_CHANGE_TITLE=Include the FROM directive in Docker history Closes #2391 (second attempt) -e BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot -e CIRRUS_BASE_SHA=0a8dc1f8064c573654f9cbd886a7596c5eac6f83 -e CIRRUS_REPO_NAME=buildah -e CIRRUS_REPO_CLONE_URL=https://github.com/containers/buildah.git -e CIRRUS_PR_DRAFT=false -e CIRRUS_TASK_ID=5436025625378816 -e CIRRUS_WORKING_DIR=/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah -e CIRRUS_CHANGE_IN_REPO=2422629d54b12cd9ac242860bbdfa01bbe9d635c -e CI=true -e CIRRUS_OS=linux -e CIRRUS_LAST_GREEN_CHANGE=fce9668d4f44d11a526be0361096e0c1e5bfd0e8 -e CIRRUS_TASK_NAME=cirrus-ci/required/in_podman -e CIRRUS_BRANCH=pull/2520 --net=host --net=container:registry --security-opt label=disable --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --cap-add=all -e GOPATH=/var/tmp/go -e GOSRC=/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah -e IN_PODMAN=false -e CONTAINER=podman -e CGROUP_MANAGER=cgroupfs -v /root/auth:/root/auth:ro -v /sys/fs/cgroup:/sys/fs/cgroup:rw -v /dev/fuse:/dev/fuse:rw -v /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah:/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah:z --workdir /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah quay.io/libpod/in_podman:master ./contrib/cirrus/setup.sh # ./contrib/cirrus/lib.sh:247 in in_podman() [+0073s] Trying to pull docker.io/library/the... [+0074s] denied: requested access to the resource is denied [+0074s] Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/the... [+0074s] manifest unknown: manifest unknown [+0074s] Trying to pull quay.io/the... [+0074s] error parsing HTTP 404 response body: invalid character '<' looking for beginning of value: "\n404 Not Found\n

Not Found

\n

The requested URL was not found on the server. If you entered the URL manually please check your spelling and try again.

\n" [+0074s] Error: unable to pull the: 3 errors occurred: [+0074s] * Error initializing source docker://the:latest: Error reading manifest latest in docker.io/library/the: errors: [+0074s] denied: requested access to the resource is denied [+0074s] unauthorized: authentication required [+0074s] [+0074s] * Error initializing source docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/the:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.fedoraproject.org/the: manifest unknown: manifest unknown [+0074s] * Error initializing source docker://quay.io/the:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/the: error parsing HTTP 404 response body: invalid character '<' looking for beginning of value: "\n404 Not Found\n

Not Found

\n

The requested URL was not found on the server. If you entered the URL manually please check your spelling and try again.

\n" [+0074s] ``` Any pointers into what could be going on here?

I did added the entry to the History through this line:


```
// Build history notes in the image configurations.
	appendHistory := func(history []v1.History) {
		currTime := time.Now()
		created := &currTime

		parent := i.parentImage
		logrus.Debugf("Adding parent image for the build = %s\n", parent)
		onews := v1.History{
			Created:    created,
			CreatedBy:  parent,
			Author:     "",
			Comment:    "FROM parent image:",
			EmptyLayer: true,
		}
		oimage.History = append(oimage.History, onews)

		dnews := docker.V2S2History{
			Created:    *created,
			CreatedBy:  parent,
			Author:     "",
			Comment:    "FROM parent image:",
			EmptyLayer: true,
		}
```

That error looks like it's coming from still attempting to pass the CIRRUS_CHANGE_TITLE variable we get in our environment into a container without quoting it, which we added a workaround for in #2553.  I think rebasing onto master will be enough here.
Ok let me retry that, thanks @nalind :+1:
